item.description: Centcom said it carried out a ‘proportional response to unjustified Iranian aggression’ after President Trump said the Iranian regime shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter. Two crew members were rescued by an unmanned drone boat. NBC News’ Courtney Kube reports.

media:content.media:title: U.S. launches ‘self-defense strikes’ against Iran after downing of helicopter
media:content.media:description: Centcom said it carried out a ‘proportional response to unjustified Iranian aggression’ after President Trump said the Iranian regime shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter. Two crew members were rescued by an unmanned drone boat. NBC News’ Courtney Kube reports.

item.title: U.S. launches ‘self-defense strikes’ against Iran after downing of helicopter

item.description: Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner is poised to win a primary election tonight as he’s facing a series of scandals. NBC News’ Ryan Nobles reports.

media:content.media:title: Key test for Democrats in Maine
media:content.media:description: Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner is poised to win a primary election tonight as he’s facing a series of scandals. NBC News’ Ryan Nobles reports.

item.title: Key test for Democrats in Maine

item.dc:creator: Adam Edelman
item.description: NBC News projected Tuesday that Hilton, a former Fox News host, will advance from a crowded all-party primary field to the general election.

media:content.media:description: Steve Hilton and Xavier Becerra

item.title: Republican Steve Hilton to face Democrat Xavier Becerra in California governor’s race

item.dc:creator: Allan Smith
item.description: NBC News projects that the state’s lieutenant governor and attorney general will face off June 23 after no candidate won more than 50% of the vote.

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump and Lt Governor Pamela Evette stand on stage during an election night watch party at the State Fairgrounds on February 24, 2024 in Columbia, South Carolina.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump issued a late endorsement to Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette in the South Carolina governor’s race.

media:content.media:text: SCGOP gubernatorial debate with US Rep Nancy Mace, U.S. Rep. Ralph Norman, State Attorney General Alan Wilson, and Rom Reddy at Wofford College in Spartanburg, South Carolina, Tuesday, May 26, 2026.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump issued a late endorsement to Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette in the South Carolina governor’s race.

item.title: Trump-backed Pamela Evette advances to GOP primary runoff against Alan Wilson in South Carolina governor’s race

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
item.description: Graham spent millions to defeat a primary opponent, NBC News projects, after receiving President Donald Trump’s endorsement last year.

media:content.media:description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., on Capitol Hill in 2023.

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham wins GOP primary, defeating wealthy challenger in South Carolina

item.dc:creator: Suzanne Gamboa
Maria Guerrero, NBC Dallas-Fort Worth
Dennis Romero
item.description: Austin Metcalf was fatally stabbed in April 2025 during a districtwide track meet in Frisco, a Dallas suburb.

media:content.media:text: Karmelo Anthony leaves jail on April 14, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Karmelo Anthony leaves jail on April 14, 2025.

media:content.media:text: Police in Frisco, Texas have charged a 17-year-old student with murder after he fatally stabbed another 17-year-old student
media:content.media:description: A 17-year-old student fatally stabbed another 17-year-old student at a high school track meet.
Austin Metcalf.

media:content.media:text: The district attorney points at Karmelo Anthony, center, at the defense table in opening arguments in McKinney, Texas, on Thursday.
media:content.media:description: The district attorney points at Karmelo Anthony, center, at the defense table in opening arguments in McKinney, Texas, on Thursday.

item.title: Karmelo Anthony sentenced to 35 years after murder conviction in Texas high school stabbing

item.description: In our series 'The Cost of Denial' NBC News’ Kate Snow meets with the parents who decided to keep their daughter in a specialized mental health care facility despite knowing it didn’t take insurance. They hoped they’d work out a deal for coverage but now find themselves more than a million dollars in debt. They believed that without the treatment, their daughter would die.

media:content.media:title: A family’s desperate choice: the massive debt to save their daughter
media:content.media:description: In our series 'The Cost of Denial' NBC News’ Kate Snow meets with the parents who decided to keep their daughter in a specialized mental health care facility despite knowing it didn’t take insurance. They hoped they’d work out a deal for coverage but now find themselves more than a million dollars in debt. They believed that without the treatment, their daughter would die.

item.title: A family’s desperate choice: the massive debt to save their daughter

item.dc:creator: Scott Wong
item.description: The legislation, passed only with Republican votes after Democrats demanded reforms to the immigration agencies, now heads to President Trump for his signature.

media:content.media:text: Image: ***BESTPIX*** Federal Agents Descend On Minneapolis For Immigration Enforcement Operations
media:content.media:description: Border Patrol agents take a man into custody in Minneapolis on Jan. 11.

item.title: Republicans pass bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol through the end of Trump’s term

item.description: NASA has announced the new crew of astronauts that will take part in the Artemis III mission to test new moon landers. Randy Bresnik, Luca Parmitano, Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas are scheduled to launch into Earth orbit next year to test out at least one of the commercially developed lunar landers expected to carry NASA astronauts to the surface of the moon in 2028.

media:content.media:title: NASA reveals the new Artemis III crew
media:content.media:description: NASA has announced the new crew of astronauts that will take part in the Artemis III mission to test new moon landers. Randy Bresnik, Luca Parmitano, Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas are scheduled to launch into Earth orbit next year to test out at least one of the commercially developed lunar landers expected to carry NASA astronauts to the surface of the moon in 2028.

item.title: NASA reveals the new Artemis III crew

item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup was expected to deliver a massive tourism and hotel boom across the U.S., but many hotels across host cities say the economic payoff is falling short of expectations — despite millions of tickets already being sold.

media:content.media:title: The World Cup hotel boom may not be happening — and neither may the expected economic windfall
media:content.media:description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup was expected to deliver a massive tourism and hotel boom across the U.S., but many hotels across host cities say the economic payoff is falling short of expectations — despite millions of tickets already being sold.

item.title: The World Cup hotel boom may not be happening — and neither may the expected economic windfall

item.description: In just one week, the 2026 FIFA World Cup is coming to North America for the first time in more than 30 years and the United States men’s national team is looking to stretch the limits of homefield advantage. It will be the final World Cup appearance for Portugal’s Cristiano Ronaldo and possibly Lionel Messi of Argentina as well. NBC’s Stephanie Gosk reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Soccer Fever Kicks in Across US Ahead of 2026 FIFA World Cup
media:content.media:description: In just one week, the 2026 FIFA World Cup is coming to North America for the first time in more than 30 years and the United States men’s national team is looking to stretch the limits of homefield advantage. It will be the final World Cup appearance for Portugal’s Cristiano Ronaldo and possibly Lionel Messi of Argentina as well. NBC’s Stephanie Gosk reports for TODAY.

item.title: Soccer Fever Kicks in Across US Ahead of 2026 FIFA World Cup

item.dc:creator: Kayla Hayempour
item.description: Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Runway Show models say every body is a target online

media:content.media:text: Ilona Maher, Ella Halikas and Remi Bader walking in Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Runway Show.
media:content.media:description: Ilona Maher; Ella Halikas; Remi Bader.

item.title: Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Runway Show models say every body is a target online

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: Adam Edelman
item.description: Nevada Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo and Democrat Aaron Ford have won their primaries, NBC News projects, officially setting up what’s expected to be one of the most competitive gubernatorial contests in the fall

media:content.media:text: A split composite of Aaron Ford and Joe Lombardo.
media:content.media:description: Nevada Democratic Attorney General Aaron Ford and Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo advanced to the general election.

item.title: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo to face off against Democrat Aaron Ford in Nevada’s race for governor

item.dc:creator: Annelise Hanson
item.description: Democratic Gov. Janet Mills is barred by term limits from running again, leading to a wide-open race.

media:content.media:text: Dr. Nirav Shah, new director of the Maine Centers for Disease Control
media:content.media:description: Physician Nirav Shah is one of the leading candidates in the Maine Democratic gubernatorial primary.

item.title: Maine’s race for governor heads to ranked choice tallies in both primaries

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
item.description: Republicans want to flip a pro-Trump, Democratic-held House seat as part of their plans to hold onto their slim majority in the House.

media:content.media:description: Former Maine Gov. Paul LePage in Bangor on May 14.

item.title: Democrats await ranked choice vote in Maine to decide key House nominee

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
item.description: Graham Platner and Susan Collins handily won their party nominations in Tuesday’s primary, setting up a marquee race with unique dynamics and major implications for control of the Senate.

media:content.media:text: Senator Bernie Sanders Holds Fighting Oligarchy Event With Senate Candidate Graham Platner.
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner on May 25, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Susan Collins speaks while seated during a hearing
media:content.media:description: Sen. Susan Collins, R-Maine, on June 2.

item.title: Maine voters set up a Senate showdown: Graham Platner versus Susan Collins

item.dc:creator: Allan Smith
item.description: NBC News projects that the state’s lieutenant governor and attorney general will face off June 23 after no candidate won more than 50% of the vote.

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump and Lt Governor Pamela Evette stand on stage during an election night watch party at the State Fairgrounds on February 24, 2024 in Columbia, South Carolina.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump issued a late endorsement to Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette in the South Carolina governor’s race.

media:content.media:text: SCGOP gubernatorial debate with US Rep Nancy Mace, U.S. Rep. Ralph Norman, State Attorney General Alan Wilson, and Rom Reddy at Wofford College in Spartanburg, South Carolina, Tuesday, May 26, 2026.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump issued a late endorsement to Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette in the South Carolina governor’s race.

item.title: Trump-backed Pamela Evette advances to GOP primary runoff against Alan Wilson in South Carolina governor’s race

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
item.description: Graham spent millions to defeat a primary opponent, NBC News projects, after receiving President Donald Trump’s endorsement last year.

media:content.media:description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., on Capitol Hill in 2023.

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham wins GOP primary, defeating wealthy challenger in South Carolina

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
item.description: The billionaire investor also spent more than $300 million of his own money on a failed bid for president in 2020.

media:content.media:description: Democratic gubernatorial candidate Tom Steyer speaks during an election night watch party on June 2, 2026, in San Francisco.

item.title: More than $200 million later, Tom Steyer’s second bid for elected office is done

item.dc:creator: Adam Edelman
item.description: NBC News projected Tuesday that Hilton, a former Fox News host, will advance from a crowded all-party primary field to the general election.

media:content.media:description: Steve Hilton and Xavier Becerra

item.title: Republican Steve Hilton to face Democrat Xavier Becerra in California governor’s race

item.dc:creator: Reuters
item.description: Hundreds of anti-immigrant protesters took to the streets of Belfast on Tuesday, with some setting vehicles alight, after police charged a Sudanese man over a knife attack that left one person with serious neck and head wounds.

media:content.media:description: Masked protesters stand by burning trash containers on Ligoniel Road in Belfast, Northern Ireland, on Tuesday, June 9, 2026, after the arrest of a Sudanese man accused of stabbing a man in the northern part of the city.
Vehicles set on fire by protesters in Belfast on Tuesday.

item.title: Violent anti-immigrant protests flare up across Belfast after knife attack

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
Julie Tsirkin
Brennan Leach
Gabrielle Khoriaty
item.description: FISA Section 702, a powerful surveillance tool, faces a risk of expiring this week after President Donald Trump named Bill Pulte as acting national intelligence director.

media:content.media:description: Bill Pulte on Jan. 12.

item.title: Trump’s elevating Bill Pulte as intelligence chief could mean FISA spy power expires

item.dc:creator: Aria Bendix
Jason Kane
Kate Snow
item.description: A specialized psychiatric treatment program helped this 24-year-old with her complex OCD. Now, her family owes the facility more than $1 million.

media:content.media:description: Larry and Kandy Levasseur with their younger daughter, Ellie, left. The Levasseurs owe more than $1 million for the cost of psychiatric care for their older daughter, Rachel.
Larry and Kandy Levasseur with their younger daughter, Ellie, left. The Levasseurs owe more than $1 million for the cost of psychiatric care for their older daughter, Rachel.
Larry and Kandy Levasseur with their younger daughter, Ellie, left. The Levasseurs owe more than $1 million for the cost of psychiatric care for their older daughter, Rachel.
Larry and Kandy Levasseur with their younger daughter, Ellie, left. The Levasseurs owe more than $1 million for the cost of psychiatric care for their older daughter, Rachel.
Larry and Kandy Levasseur with their younger daughter, Ellie, left. The Levasseurs owe more than $1 million for the cost of psychiatric care for their older daughter, Rachel.
Larry and Kandy Levasseur with their younger daughter, Ellie, left. The Levasseurs owe more than $1 million for the cost of psychiatric care for their older daughter, Rachel.
Larry and Kandy Levasseur with their younger daughter, Ellie, left. The Levasseurs owe more than $1 million for the cost of psychiatric care for their older daughter, Rachel.

media:content.media:text: A triptych of details of her room.
media:content.media:description: Larry and Kandy Levasseur with their younger daughter, Ellie, left. The Levasseurs owe more than $1 million for the cost of psychiatric care for their older daughter, Rachel.
Larry and Kandy Levasseur with their younger daughter, Ellie, left. The Levasseurs owe more than $1 million for the cost of psychiatric care for their older daughter, Rachel.

item.title: After years of failed treatments, this psych program finally worked. Now her family owes $1 million.

item.dc:creator: Tim Stelloh
Lindsay Good
item.description: A couple sustained significant injuries in a black bear attack in California’s Sierra Nevada, authorities said Tuesday

media:content.media:text: An American black bear (Ursus americanus) is walking through.
media:content.media:description: An American black bear in British Columbia.

item.title: Man fights off bear with hatchet after attack in California mountains
